ddls:
CREATE TABLE "content_likes" (
  "like_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each like */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content liked */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who liked the content */,
  "like_date" TEXT /* Date the content was liked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("like_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_followers" (
  "follower_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each follower */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being followed */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date the user was followed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("follower_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_following" (
  "following_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each following */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user doing the following */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date the user was followed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("following_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who rated the content */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date the content was rated */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* Value of the rating (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content the tag is associated with */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_categories" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content item */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the content belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_comments_likes" (
  "like_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each like */,
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the comment liked */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who liked the comment */,
  "like_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was liked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("like_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
column_names: ID of the user who liked the content
ID of the user being followed
ID of the user doing the following
ID of the user who rated the content
ID of the content the tag is associated with
ID of the category the content belongs to
ID of the user who liked the comment
ID of the role assigned to the user
